title: Muhlenbergia willdenowii
taxonomy: Muhlenbergia willdenowii is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. Specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Festucoideae and the tribe Stipeae. This classification is based on its physical characteristics, such as its leaf structure and inflorescence arrangement. Within the genus Muhlenbergia, it is one of the 65 recognized species. Its classification is also influenced by its genetic relationships with other grass species.

intro: Muhlenbergia willdenowii, also known as Willdenow's Muhly or Mexican Hair Grass, is a perennial grass native to Mexico and the southwestern United States, characterized by its delicate, feathery plumes and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Muhlenbergia willdenowii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Muhlenbergia willdenowii is often prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as being used in the form of tinctures or infusions to treat respiratory issues and other ailments.
